metadata.audit_notes: Re-audited 2026-06-19. Removed #e6ff00 chartreuse from accent — it is defined as a CSS variable but never visually rendered on the marketing site. Real brand accent is warm sand #d7c8af used for button shadows, borders, and subtle warmth.

primary.usage: Primary brand color used for main text, buttons, and key UI elements

secondary.usage: Navigation links, secondary text, muted UI elements

accent.usage: Warm sand accent used for CTA button borders, box shadows, warm border tints, and subtle emphasis throughout the site

text.usage: Default body text and foreground color

palette.name: gray-100
palette.usage_context: --color-gray-100, --color-surface-active, active surface state, nav button bg variant

palette.name: white
palette.usage_context: --color-white, backgrounds, primary-foreground text on dark surfaces

palette.name: surface
palette.usage_context: --color-surface, --color-sand-50, footer background, login side panel, section backgrounds

palette.name: warm-sand
palette.usage_context: --color-shadow-warm, --color-shadow-sand, warm box shadow and border tints on CTA buttons, primary brand accent

palette.name: text-primary
palette.usage_context: rgb(17,17,17), heading text, active nav link text, CTA button text

defined_but_unused.name: chartreuse
defined_but_unused.note: Defined in CSS variables but never visually rendered on the marketing site. Not used for any visible element, button, badge, or accent. Excluded from templates to avoid misleading brand representation.

header.description: Flywheel icon mark (stylized overlapping curves forming a flywheel shape) + 'Flywheel' text wordmark, rendered in black on white

footer.description: Large 'FLYWHEEL' display type rendered in very large weight across the full width of the footer, uppercase, in a muted color on the warm surface background
footer.style: uppercase, full-width display, weight 700, color approx rgba(0,0,0,0.06) on #faf9f6 background

gradients.name: footer-fade
gradients.usage: Transition from main white background to warm surface footer area

gradients.name: warm-shadow-glow
gradients.usage: Subtle warm glow behind CTA elements and cards

gradients.name: logo-icon-gradient
gradients.usage: Applied to the flywheel icon mark SVG via linearGradient definition

particle_scatter.description: A network/knowledge-graph-style visualization of scattered dots (circles) in varying sizes from 2px to 20px, positioned across the hero section. Dots are gray at varying opacities (roughly #b0b0b0 to #d0d0d0). Some dots are connected by very thin, faint lines. The pattern evokes data connections, knowledge graphs, and content networks — core to Flywheel's brand as an intelligent content platform.
particle_scatter.usage: Hero section background, positioned primarily in the right half of the viewport

flywheel_watermark.description: The word 'FLYWHEEL' rendered in very large (approx 150-200px), bold uppercase type across the footer, barely visible (opacity ~0.04-0.06) on the warm surface background. Creates a branded watermark effect.

warm_cta_button.description: CTA buttons with transparent background, 1px border in rgba(215,200,175,0.5), warm sand box-shadow, no border-radius. The warm sand creates an elegant, premium feel rather than an aggressive bright accent.
warm_cta_button.usage: Book a Call / Get in touch CTAs

styles.box-shadow: rgba(215, 200, 175, 0.25) 0px 4px 20px 0px, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.06) 0px 1px 4px 0px, rgba(255, 252, 248, 0.5) 0px 1px 1px 0px inset, rgba(215, 200, 175, 0.15) 0px -1px 1px 0px inset

design_portfolio.philosophy: Flywheel's visual identity is rooted in editorial restraint and Swiss-design precision. Every composition should feel like a page from a premium tech publication, not a marketing deck. The brand communicates authority through what it removes, not what it adds. Negative space is the primary design element.
composition_rules: 60%+ of the canvas must be negative space (white or warm off-white). Content should breathe.
Maximum 3 focal elements per graphic: typically heading + one supporting element + brand mark.
Left-heavy content placement: primary text anchored to the left third, right side reserved for decorative or abstract elements.
Asymmetric balance: never center everything. Offset headings, stagger elements, create visual tension through intentional imbalance.
Single axis of alignment: all content elements should share one strong alignment edge (usually left).
Vertical rhythm via generous gaps: 24-48px between content blocks, never cramped.
The canvas edge is sacred: maintain 48-80px padding from all edges, content never crowds the frame.

visual_density.description: Extremely sparse. Flywheel compositions are defined by what is absent. A typical section on their site contains one heading, one short paragraph, and nothing else. The brand treats emptiness as a design element that conveys confidence and clarity.
constraints: No more than 3 text blocks visible at once (heading, subtext, eyebrow/caption).
If a layout has data points or list items, limit to 4-7 items maximum, each under 12 words.
Decorative elements (dots, lines, watermarks) must be at opacity 0.03-0.20, never competing with content.
Every element must have a clear purpose. If removing an element doesn't reduce comprehension, remove it.

hierarchy_rules.description: Stark, dramatic size contrast between heading and body text. The heading dominates; everything else recedes. This creates an unmistakable focal point and editorial gravitas.
rules: Headlines: 42-60px, weight 600, letter-spacing -0.03em to -0.04em. Always the dominant element.
Subtext: 16-20px, weight 400, color #555 or #666. Deliberately subdued.
Eyebrow/label text: 11-13px, uppercase, letter-spacing 0.06-0.08em, DM Mono or Inter weight 500, color #888 or #999.
Data/stat numbers: 36-48px, weight 600, DM Mono for technical feel.
Never let two elements compete for attention. One element dominates, rest support.

particle_network.description: Scattered dots (circles) in varying sizes from 2-8px, positioned in the right half or periphery of the canvas. Some connected by hairline strokes. Represents intelligent content connections and knowledge graphs. Always subtle, never dominant.
particle_network.usage: Background texture, typically right-biased or corner-placed

warm_sand_shadow.description: CTA elements and featured cards use rgba(215,200,175,0.25) box-shadow with 4-20px blur. Creates a warm, premium glow distinct from typical drop shadows.

square_geometry.description: Border-radius 0px on buttons, badges, stat tiles, and number markers. Square edges are a signature. The only rounded elements are the particle dots themselves.

warm_accent_bar.description: A thin (2-3px) horizontal bar of warm sand (#d7c8af) at the top of a card or as a divider. Fades to transparent via gradient. A subtle signature element.

flywheel_watermark.description: The word 'FLYWHEEL' rendered at extreme size (120-160px), weight 700, uppercase, at opacity 0.02-0.04. Positioned behind main content as a barely-visible brand imprint.
flywheel_watermark.usage: Optional, for bold_statement and centered compositions only

warm_gradient_footer.description: A subtle gradient from transparent to rgba(215,200,175,0.05) at the bottom 80px of the canvas. Adds warmth without visible color.

prohibitions: NO gradients on text. Text is always solid color.
NO colorful icons or emoji. Visual elements are monochrome (black, gray, warm sand only).
NO illustrations or cartoon-style graphics. Abstraction only through geometry and dots.
NO rounded cards or pill-shaped buttons. Square geometry is the signature.
NO bright or saturated colors. The palette is black, white, gray scale, and warm sand.
NO busy or cluttered layouts. If it feels full, remove elements until it breathes.
NO dark or black backgrounds for full cards. Backgrounds are white (#fff) or warm off-white (#faf9f6).
NO drop shadows with blue or cool tones. Only warm sand shadows.
NO all-caps headings (only labels and brand marks are uppercase).
NO chartreuse, neon, or #e6ff00. This color is defined in CSS but never used visually.

tone.description: Editorial, premium, Swiss-design influenced, restrained, intellectual. The visual voice is that of a confident publication that doesn't need to shout. Compositions should feel considered and deliberate, as if every pixel placement was intentional.
analogies: A premium tech journal, not a SaaS landing page
An architect's portfolio, not a marketing brochure
A museum exhibit label, not a conference slide
tone.emotional_register: Quiet confidence, intellectual clarity, refined simplicity

left_anchored_hero.description: Heading left-aligned at 80px from left edge, occupying ~55% of width. Right side has particle dots or is empty. CTA or subtext below heading. Used for primary messaging.

centered_statement.description: Single bold statement centered on canvas. FLYWHEEL watermark behind at very low opacity. Warm glow behind text. Minimal: just the statement, a thin accent underline, and optional subtext.

structured_data.description: Left heading with right-side data grid or vertical list. Clean separation between narrative (left) and evidence (right). Data uses DM Mono for numbers.
structured_data.separator: thin vertical line or generous gap

stacked_list.description: Heading at top, followed by vertically stacked items with consistent spacing. Each item has a square number marker or bullet. Maximum 7 items. Clean left alignment throughout.

reference_compositions.name: Homepage Hero
reference_compositions.description: Massive 4-line heading 'Intelligent Content that Drives Revenue.' left-aligned, occupying the left 45% of the viewport. Right half filled with scattered particle dots (varying sizes, connected by faint lines). Single square CTA button 'Book a Call' with warm sand shadow below the heading. 60%+ negative space. Navigation bar above is minimal: logo, 3 links, login, CTA.

reference_compositions.name: Value Proposition Section
reference_compositions.description: Centered heading 'Your Content Needs a Flywheel.' with a single line of subdued subtext below. Below that, a 4-column grid of brief descriptions (each: bold title + 1-2 line description + 'Learn More' link). Enormous top/bottom padding. No decorative elements. Pure typography.

reference_compositions.name: Feature Showcase
reference_compositions.description: Left side: section number in muted text, bold heading, body paragraph. Right side: subtle product screenshot or abstract UI representation. Asymmetric split (~45/55). Thin horizontal rule separating from previous section.

reference_compositions.name: Bottom CTA Section
reference_compositions.description: Centered heading 'Ready to build your content flywheel?' with subtext and a single CTA button. Below: the word 'FLYWHEEL' spanning full width at barely-visible opacity (~0.05). Warm surface background (#faf9f6). Absolute minimum elements.
